target.title: fix(doctor): archive conflicting plugin install index
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: legacy `plugins/installs.json` can keep triggering post-upgrade migration warnings after `openclaw doctor --fix` when the shared SQLite install record already has the authoritative resolved npm artifact metadata, but broad archival of every conflict would hide real metadata drift.
- Solution: archive the legacy JSON only when the current SQLite npm record proves it supersedes the legacy selector: same package name, same resolved version, exact resolved spec, and registry artifact metadata such as integrity or shasum. Leave unresolved conflicts on the existing warning/source path.
- What changed: narrowed the installed-plugin-index migration predicate in `src/infra/state-migrations.ts` and updated `src/commands/doctor-state-migrations.test.ts` to cover both the safe archival path and true-conflict preservation.
- What did NOT change: missing legacy records still merge into SQLite, invalid legacy JSON still stays in place, matching richer records still archive, and unresolved package/spec drift still stays visible for operator or maintainer review.

Fixes #90213

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** Repeated legacy state migration warnings after `openclaw doctor --fix` or later OpenClaw commands when `plugins/installs.json` disagrees with shared SQLite install metadata that already contains the resolved npm artifact record.
- **Real environment tested:** Local Linux checkout using the real OpenClaw state migration implementation, real shared SQLite installed-plugin index store, and temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` fixtures for `brave`/`lobster` npm records plus an unresolved `demo` conflict.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```bash
  env -C "$TASK_WORKTREE" node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/commands/doctor-state-migrations.test.ts src/infra/state-migrations.state-dir.test.ts src/cli/program/config-guard.test.ts
  env -C "$TASK_WORKTREE" corepack pnpm@11.2.2 exec oxlint src/infra/state-migrations.ts src/commands/doctor-state-migrations.test.ts
  env -C "$TASK_WORKTREE" corepack pnpm@11.2.2 tsgo:core
  env -C "$TASK_WORKTREE" git diff --check
  env -C "$TASK_WORKTREE" OPENCLAW_REPO_ROOT="$TASK_WORKTREE" PROOF_RUN_LABEL=patched-pr-head-eead5be9b5 node --import tsx "$EVIDENCE_DIR/narrowed-plugin-index-warning-proof.ts"
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:**
  Latest main before the patch still repeats the legacy install-index warning and leaves the legacy source in place:
  ```text
  PROOF_RUN_LABEL=latest-main-fffd72f36d
  FIRST_WARNINGS=["Left plugin install index in place because shared SQLite state has conflicting plugin install metadata for: brave, lobster"]
  FIRST_CHANGES=[]
  LEGACY_SOURCE_EXISTS_AFTER_FIRST=true
  LEGACY_ARCHIVE_EXISTS_AFTER_FIRST=false
  SECOND_WARNINGS=["Left plugin install index in place because shared SQLite state has conflicting plugin install metadata for: brave, lobster"]
  SECOND_CHANGES=[]
  CANONICAL_BRAVE_SPEC=brave@latest
  CANONICAL_LOBSTER_SPEC=lobster@latest
  ```

  Patched PR head archives only the proven-safe resolved npm artifact case, then the second run is quiet:
  ```text
  PROOF_RUN_LABEL=patched-pr-head-eead5be9b5
  SAFE_ARTIFACT_FIRST_WARNINGS=[]
  SAFE_ARTIFACT_FIRST_CHANGES=["Archived plugin install index legacy source → /tmp/openclaw-90213-SAFE_ARTIFACT-c19jNE/plugins/installs.json.migrated"]
  SAFE_ARTIFACT_LEGACY_SOURCE_EXISTS_AFTER_FIRST=false
  SAFE_ARTIFACT_LEGACY_ARCHIVE_EXISTS_AFTER_FIRST=true
  SAFE_ARTIFACT_SECOND_WARNINGS=[]
  SAFE_ARTIFACT_SECOND_CHANGES=[]
  SAFE_ARTIFACT_CANONICAL_SPECS={"brave":"brave@latest","lobster":"lobster@latest"}
  ```

  The same patched PR head keeps unresolved selector/spec drift visible instead of archiving it:
  ```text
  UNRESOLVED_CONFLICT_FIRST_WARNINGS=["Left plugin install index in place because shared SQLite state has conflicting plugin install metadata for: demo"]
  UNRESOLVED_CONFLICT_FIRST_CHANGES=[]
  UNRESOLVED_CONFLICT_LEGACY_SOURCE_EXISTS_AFTER_FIRST=true
  UNRESOLVED_CONFLICT_LEGACY_ARCHIVE_EXISTS_AFTER_FIRST=false
  UNRESOLVED_CONFLICT_SECOND_WARNINGS=["Left plugin install index in place because shared SQLite state has conflicting plugin install metadata for: demo"]
  UNRESOLVED_CONFLICT_SECOND_CHANGES=[]
  UNRESOLVED_CONFLICT_CANONICAL_SPECS={"demo":"demo@latest"}
  ```

  Regression tests and local checks:
  ```text
  env -C "$TASK_WORKTREE" node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/commands/doctor-state-migrations.test.ts src/infra/state-migrations.state-dir.test.ts src/cli/program/config-guard.test.ts
  [test] passed 3 Vitest shards in 109.82s

  env -C "$TASK_WORKTREE" corepack pnpm@11.2.2 exec oxlint src/infra/state-migrations.ts src/commands/doctor-state-migrations.test.ts
  # no output

  env -C "$TASK_WORKTREE" corepack pnpm@11.2.2 tsgo:core
  $ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo

  env -C "$TASK_WORKTREE" git diff --check
  # no output
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** SQLite-canonical npm records with exact resolved artifact metadata archive `plugins/installs.json` on the first migration and stop repeating warnings on the second run, while unresolved conflicts keep the legacy source in place and continue surfacing the existing warning.
- **What was not tested:** No globally installed npm package, live PiClaw upgrade, packaged v2026.6.1 upgrade, real Brave/Lobster plugin activation, external Brave Search API calls, LM Studio, or provider/model traffic was exercised.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: state migration unit/integration coverage plus real source-runtime proof with temporary state directories.
- Target test file: `src/commands/doctor-state-migrations.test.ts`.
- Scenario locked in: resolved npm artifact metadata now archives legacy `plugins/installs.json` and a follow-up migration run is quiet; unresolved package/spec drift remains warning-visible and unarchived.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it exercises the same doctor migration entry point and persisted SQLite index store that produce the repeated warning without needing external plugins or credentials.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: `migrateLegacyInstalledPluginIndex` treated all legacy/current install-record conflicts the same, so safe superseded npm artifact records could not reach the existing archive step without also broadening archival for true conflicts.
- Missing detection / guardrail: the old regression coverage did not distinguish proven-safe resolved artifact metadata from unresolved install metadata drift.
target.author: mushuiyu886
